steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Text Structure question: you must identify how the passage is organized from beginning to end. The decisive clue is the word “But” opening the final sentence, signaling a contrast between the belief stated earlier and what the study actually found.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It explains how PAs typically affect economic development in the regions where they are established, summarizes the findings of several economic studies that support this explanation, and then concedes that a particular PA is an exception to the general trend.: The passage never presents any study supporting the claim that PAs hinder development — the study does the opposite. The passage makes no “exception to a general trend” framing. ✗
  • Choice B — It introduces a widely held belief about the effect PAs have on the economy, proposes a reason for that belief's prevalence, and then details a study whose findings seemingly conflict with that belief.: Sentence 1 introduces the belief (PAs hinder development). Sentence 2 explains why it persists (assessments miss indirect tourism effects). Sentence 3 (“But”) presents Narayan et al.’s finding that tourism actually boosts nearby economic sectors — conflicting with the belief. ✓
  • Choice C — It makes a generalization about the difficulty of using PAs to reconcile economic and conservation priorities, demonstrates why most PAs fall short of achieving that goal, and then holds up a particular PA as an example of how that goal can be achieved.: The passage says nothing about reconciling conservation and economic priorities as a goal — it is about a widespread belief and one study’s contradictory finding. ✗
  • Choice D — It states a hypothesis that accounts for why PAs generally negatively impact local economies, outlines how a research team's findings support that hypothesis, and then suggests how a novel methodology led a separate team of researchers to a different conclusion.: Only one research team (Narayan et al.) is mentioned, not two. Their findings contradict the belief rather than support it. ✗
That matches Choice B — It introduces a widely held belief about the effect PAs have on the economy, proposes a reason for that belief's prevalence, and then details a study whose findings seemingly conflict with that belief..
